MaxAbsEStateIndex.label: Maximum Absolute Electrotopological State Index
MaxAbsEStateIndex.description: The largest absolute value of the atomic Electrotopological State (E-State) indices in the molecule.

MaxEStateIndex.label: Maximum Electrotopological State Index
MaxEStateIndex.description: The highest, most positive, atomic Electrotopological State index in the molecule.

MinAbsEStateIndex.label: Minimum Absolute Electrotopological State Index
MinAbsEStateIndex.description: The smallest absolute value of the atomic Electrotopological State indices in the molecule.

MinEStateIndex.label: Minimum Electrotopological State Index
MinEStateIndex.description: The lowest atomic Electrotopological State index in the molecule (can be negative for some molecules).

FpDensityMorgan1.label: Morgan Fingerprint Density (Radius 1)
FpDensityMorgan1.description: The number of non-zero bits in the Morgan fingerprint with radius 1 divided by the number of heavy atoms. It measures the density of unique local atomic environments at radius 1.

FpDensityMorgan2.label: Morgan Fingerprint Density (Radius 2)
FpDensityMorgan2.description: The number of non-zero bits in the Morgan fingerprint with radius 2 divided by the number of heavy atoms. It reflects the structural diversity of atomic environments extending up to two bonds.

FpDensityMorgan3.label: Morgan Fingerprint Density (Radius 3)
FpDensityMorgan3.description: The number of non-zero bits in the Morgan fingerprint with radius 3 divided by the number of heavy atoms. It characterizes the complexity of larger local structural environments within the molecule.

BCUT2D_MWHI.label: Highest BCUT Eigenvalue Weighted by Atomic Mass
BCUT2D_MWHI.description: The largest eigenvalue of the two-dimensional BCUT matrix constructed using atomic masses as atom weights. It reflects the distribution of heavy atoms within the molecular topology.

BCUT2D_MWLOW.label: Lowest BCUT Eigenvalue Weighted by Atomic Mass
BCUT2D_MWLOW.description: The smallest eigenvalue of the two-dimensional BCUT matrix constructed using atomic masses as atom weights. It describes the lower bound of the mass-weighted topological distribution.

BCUT2D_CHGHI.label: Highest BCUT Eigenvalue Weighted by Atomic Partial Charge
BCUT2D_CHGHI.description: The largest eigenvalue of the two-dimensional BCUT matrix constructed using atomic partial charges as atom weights. It captures the upper extent of the molecular charge distribution.

BCUT2D_CHGLO.label: Lowest BCUT Eigenvalue Weighted by Atomic Partial Charge
BCUT2D_CHGLO.description: The smallest eigenvalue of the two-dimensional BCUT matrix constructed using atomic partial charges as atom weights. It represents the lower extent of the molecular charge distribution.

BCUT2D_LOGPHI.label: Highest BCUT Eigenvalue Weighted by Atomic LogP Contribution
BCUT2D_LOGPHI.description: The largest eigenvalue of the two-dimensional BCUT matrix weighted by atomic Wildman–Crippen LogP contributions. It reflects the distribution of hydrophobic character across the molecular graph.

BCUT2D_LOGPLOW.label: Lowest BCUT Eigenvalue Weighted by Atomic LogP Contribution
BCUT2D_LOGPLOW.description: The smallest eigenvalue of the two-dimensional BCUT matrix weighted by atomic Wildman–Crippen LogP contributions. It characterizes the lower bound of hydrophobicity distribution within the molecule.

BCUT2D_MRHI.label: Highest BCUT Eigenvalue Weighted by Atomic Molar Refractivity Contribution
BCUT2D_MRHI.description: The largest eigenvalue of the two-dimensional BCUT matrix weighted by atomic Wildman–Crippen molar refractivity contributions. It describes the upper extent of molecular polarizability distribution.

BCUT2D_MRLOW.label: Lowest BCUT Eigenvalue Weighted by Atomic Molar Refractivity Contribution
BCUT2D_MRLOW.description: The smallest eigenvalue of the two-dimensional BCUT matrix weighted by atomic Wildman–Crippen molar refractivity contributions. It reflects the lower extent of molecular polarizability distribution.

AvgIpc.label: Average Information Content
AvgIpc.description: The average graph information content (IPC) of the molecular topology. It quantifies the average structural complexity and symmetry of the molecular graph based on information theory.

MolWt.label: Molecular weight
MolWt.description: Average molecular weight calculated from naturally occurring isotopes.

ExactMolWt.label: Exact molecular weight
ExactMolWt.description: Monoisotopic molecular weight calculated using the most abundant isotope of each element.

TPSA.label: Topological polar surface area
TPSA.description: Surface area contributed by polar atoms (typically oxygen, nitrogen, sulfur, phosphorus).

MolMR.label: Molar refractivity
MolMR.description: Estimate of molecular polarizability based on atomic contributions.

FractionCSP3.label: Fraction of sp³ carbon atoms
FractionCSP3.description: Fraction of carbon atoms that are sp³ hybridized.

NumHAcceptors.label: Hydrogen-bond acceptors
NumHAcceptors.description: Number of atoms capable of accepting hydrogen bonds.

NumHDonors.label: Hydrogen-bond donors
NumHDonors.description: Number of atoms capable of donating hydrogen bonds.

NumRotatableBonds.label: Rotatable bonds
NumRotatableBonds.description: Number of freely rotatable single bonds.

NumHeteroatoms.label: Heteroatoms
NumHeteroatoms.description: Number of non-carbon and non-hydrogen atoms.

NumRings.label: Ring count
NumRings.description: Total number of rings in the molecule.

NumAromaticHeterocycles.label: Aromatic heterocycles
NumAromaticHeterocycles.description: Number of aromatic rings containing heteroatoms.

FormalCharge.label: Formal charge
FormalCharge.description: Net formal charge on the molecule.

qed.label: Drug-likeness score (QED)
qed.description: Quantitative Estimate of Drug-likeness based on medicinal chemistry properties.

LabuteASA.label: Approximate surface area
LabuteASA.description: Labute approximation of solvent-accessible surface area.

HallKierAlpha.label: Hall-Kier alpha index
HallKierAlpha.description: Correction factor used in molecular connectivity indices.

SMR_VSA2.label: Molar Refractivity van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 2)
SMR_VSA2.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Wildman–Crippen molar refractivity contributions fall within the second predefined molar refractivity interval.

SMR_VSA3.label: Molar Refractivity van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 3)
SMR_VSA3.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Wildman–Crippen molar refractivity contributions fall within the third predefined molar refractivity interval.

SMR_VSA4.label: Molar Refractivity van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 4)
SMR_VSA4.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Wildman–Crippen molar refractivity contributions fall within the fourth predefined molar refractivity interval.

SMR_VSA5.label: Molar Refractivity van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 5)
SMR_VSA5.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Wildman–Crippen molar refractivity contributions fall within the fifth predefined molar refractivity interval.

SMR_VSA6.label: Molar Refractivity van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 6)
SMR_VSA6.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Wildman–Crippen molar refractivity contributions fall within the sixth predefined molar refractivity interval.

SMR_VSA7.label: Molar Refractivity van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 7)
SMR_VSA7.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Wildman–Crippen molar refractivity contributions fall within the seventh predefined molar refractivity interval.

SMR_VSA8.label: Molar Refractivity van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 8)
SMR_VSA8.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Wildman–Crippen molar refractivity contributions fall within the eighth predefined molar refractivity interval.

SMR_VSA9.label: Molar Refractivity van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 9)
SMR_VSA9.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Wildman–Crippen molar refractivity contributions fall within the ninth predefined molar refractivity interval.

SMR_VSA10.label: Molar Refractivity van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 10)
SMR_VSA10.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Wildman–Crippen molar refractivity contributions fall within the tenth predefined molar refractivity interval.

SlogP_VSA2.label: LogP van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 2)
SlogP_VSA2.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Wildman–Crippen LogP contributions fall within the second predefined hydrophobicity interval.

SlogP_VSA3.label: LogP van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 3)
SlogP_VSA3.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Wildman–Crippen LogP contributions fall within the third predefined hydrophobicity interval.

SlogP_VSA4.label: LogP van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 4)
SlogP_VSA4.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Wildman–Crippen LogP contributions fall within the fourth predefined hydrophobicity interval.

SlogP_VSA5.label: LogP van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 5)
SlogP_VSA5.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Wildman–Crippen LogP contributions fall within the fifth predefined hydrophobicity interval.

SlogP_VSA6.label: LogP van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 6)
SlogP_VSA6.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Wildman–Crippen LogP contributions fall within the sixth predefined hydrophobicity interval.

SlogP_VSA7.label: LogP van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 7)
SlogP_VSA7.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Wildman–Crippen LogP contributions fall within the seventh predefined hydrophobicity interval.

SlogP_VSA8.label: LogP van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 8)
SlogP_VSA8.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Wildman–Crippen LogP contributions fall within the eighth predefined hydrophobicity interval.

SlogP_VSA9.label: LogP van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 9)
SlogP_VSA9.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Wildman–Crippen LogP contributions fall within the ninth predefined hydrophobicity interval.

SlogP_VSA10.label: LogP van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 10)
SlogP_VSA10.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Wildman–Crippen LogP contributions fall within the tenth predefined hydrophobicity interval.

SlogP_VSA11.label: LogP van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 11)
SlogP_VSA11.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Wildman–Crippen LogP contributions fall within the eleventh predefined hydrophobicity interval.

SlogP_VSA12.label: LogP van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 12)
SlogP_VSA12.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Wildman–Crippen LogP contributions fall within the twelfth predefined hydrophobicity interval.

Kappa3.label: Third kappa shape index
Kappa3.description: Molecular shape descriptor describing molecular shape complexity.

Chi0n.label: Zeroth-Order Molecular Connectivity Chi Index (Normal)
Chi0n.description: The zeroth-order molecular connectivity index calculated using the normal atomic connectivity values. It quantifies molecular branching without considering valence corrections.

Chi0v.label: Zeroth-Order Molecular Connectivity Chi Index (Valence)
Chi0v.description: The zeroth-order molecular connectivity index calculated using valence connectivity values. It incorporates atomic valence information into the connectivity calculation.

Chi1n.label: First-Order Molecular Connectivity Chi Index (Normal)
Chi1n.description: The first-order molecular connectivity index based on normal atomic connectivity values. It characterizes connectivity between directly bonded atoms.

Chi1v.label: First-Order Molecular Connectivity Chi Index (Valence)
Chi1v.description: The first-order molecular connectivity index calculated using valence connectivity values, reflecting both bonding topology and atomic valence.

Chi2n.label: Second-Order Molecular Connectivity Chi Index (Normal)
Chi2n.description: The second-order molecular connectivity index based on paths of length two using normal atomic connectivity values.

Chi3n.label: Third-Order Molecular Connectivity Chi Index (Normal)
Chi3n.description: The third-order molecular connectivity index based on paths of length three using normal atomic connectivity values.

Chi4n.label: Fourth-Order Molecular Connectivity Chi Index (Normal)
Chi4n.description: The fourth-order molecular connectivity index based on paths of length four using normal atomic connectivity values.

EState_VSA1.label: Electrotopological State Surface Area (Bin 1)
EState_VSA1.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Electrotopological State (E-State) values fall within the first predefined E-State interval.

EState_VSA2.label: Electrotopological State Surface Area (Bin 2)
EState_VSA2.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose E-State values fall within the second predefined E-State interval.

EState_VSA3.label: Electrotopological State Surface Area (Bin 3)
EState_VSA3.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose E-State values fall within the third predefined E-State interval.

EState_VSA4.label: Electrotopological State Surface Area (Bin 4)
EState_VSA4.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose E-State values fall within the fourth predefined E-State interval.

EState_VSA5.label: Electrotopological State Surface Area (Bin 5)
EState_VSA5.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose E-State values fall within the fifth predefined E-State interval.

EState_VSA6.label: Electrotopological State Surface Area (Bin 6)
EState_VSA6.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose E-State values fall within the sixth predefined E-State interval.

EState_VSA7.label: Electrotopological State Surface Area (Bin 7)
EState_VSA7.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose E-State values fall within the seventh predefined E-State interval.

EState_VSA8.label: Electrotopological State Surface Area (Bin 8)
EState_VSA8.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose E-State values fall within the eighth predefined E-State interval.

EState_VSA9.label: Electrotopological State Surface Area (Bin 9)
EState_VSA9.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose E-State values fall within the ninth predefined E-State interval.

EState_VSA10.label: Electrotopological State Surface Area (Bin 10)
EState_VSA10.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose E-State values fall within the tenth predefined E-State interval.

EState_VSA11.label: Electrotopological State Surface Area (Bin 11)
EState_VSA11.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose E-State values fall within the eleventh predefined E-State interval.

VSA_EState1.label: Electrotopological State Sum for van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 1)
VSA_EState1.description: Sum of the Electrotopological State (E-State) indices of atoms whose van der Waals surface areas fall within the first predefined surface-area interval.

VSA_EState2.label: Electrotopological State Sum for van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 2)
VSA_EState2.description: Sum of the E-State indices of atoms whose van der Waals surface areas fall within the second predefined surface-area interval.

VSA_EState3.label: Electrotopological State Sum for van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 3)
VSA_EState3.description: Sum of the E-State indices of atoms whose van der Waals surface areas fall within the third predefined surface-area interval.

VSA_EState4.label: Electrotopological State Sum for van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 4)
VSA_EState4.description: Sum of the E-State indices of atoms whose van der Waals surface areas fall within the fourth predefined surface-area interval.

VSA_EState5.label: Electrotopological State Sum for van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 5)
VSA_EState5.description: Sum of the E-State indices of atoms whose van der Waals surface areas fall within the fifth predefined surface-area interval.

VSA_EState6.label: Electrotopological State Sum for van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 6)
VSA_EState6.description: Sum of the E-State indices of atoms whose van der Waals surface areas fall within the sixth predefined surface-area interval.

VSA_EState7.label: Electrotopological State Sum for van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 7)
VSA_EState7.description: Sum of the E-State indices of atoms whose van der Waals surface areas fall within the seventh predefined surface-area interval.

VSA_EState8.label: Electrotopological State Sum for van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 8)
VSA_EState8.description: Sum of the E-State indices of atoms whose van der Waals surface areas fall within the eighth predefined surface-area interval.

VSA_EState9.label: Electrotopological State Sum for van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 9)
VSA_EState9.description: Sum of the E-State indices of atoms whose van der Waals surface areas fall within the ninth predefined surface-area interval.

VSA_EState10.label: Electrotopological State Sum for van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 10)
VSA_EState10.description: Sum of the E-State indices of atoms whose van der Waals surface areas fall within the tenth predefined surface-area interval.

PEOE_VSA1.label: Partial Charge van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 1)
PEOE_VSA1.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Gasteiger partial charges (PEOE) fall within the first predefined charge interval.

PEOE_VSA2.label: Partial Charge van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 2)
PEOE_VSA2.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Gasteiger partial charges fall within the second predefined charge interval.

PEOE_VSA3.label: Partial Charge van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 3)
PEOE_VSA3.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Gasteiger partial charges fall within the third predefined charge interval.

PEOE_VSA4.label: Partial Charge van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 4)
PEOE_VSA4.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Gasteiger partial charges fall within the fourth predefined charge interval.

PEOE_VSA5.label: Partial Charge van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 5)
PEOE_VSA5.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Gasteiger partial charges fall within the fifth predefined charge interval.

PEOE_VSA6.label: Partial Charge van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 6)
PEOE_VSA6.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Gasteiger partial charges fall within the sixth predefined charge interval.

PEOE_VSA7.label: Partial Charge van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 7)
PEOE_VSA7.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Gasteiger partial charges fall within the seventh predefined charge interval.

PEOE_VSA8.label: Partial Charge van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 8)
PEOE_VSA8.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Gasteiger partial charges fall within the eighth predefined charge interval.

PEOE_VSA9.label: Partial Charge van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 9)
PEOE_VSA9.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Gasteiger partial charges fall within the ninth predefined charge interval.

PEOE_VSA10.label: Partial Charge van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 10)
PEOE_VSA10.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Gasteiger partial charges fall within the tenth predefined charge interval.

PEOE_VSA11.label: Partial Charge van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 11)
PEOE_VSA11.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Gasteiger partial charges fall within the eleventh predefined charge interval.

PEOE_VSA12.label: Partial Charge van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 12)
PEOE_VSA12.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Gasteiger partial charges fall within the twelfth predefined charge interval.

PEOE_VSA13.label: Partial Charge van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 13)
PEOE_VSA13.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Gasteiger partial charges fall within the thirteenth predefined charge interval.

PEOE_VSA14.label: Partial Charge van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 14)
PEOE_VSA14.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Gasteiger partial charges fall within the fourteenth predefined charge interval.

SMR_VSA1.label: Molar Refractivity van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 1)
SMR_VSA1.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Wildman–Crippen molar refractivity contributions fall within the first predefined interval.

SlogP_VSA1.label: LogP van der Waals Surface Area (Bin 1)
SlogP_VSA1.description: Total van der Waals surface area of atoms whose Wildman–Crippen atomic LogP contributions fall within the first predefined hydrophobicity interval.

Ipc.label: Information Content Index
Ipc.description: The graph information content of the molecular topology. It measures molecular complexity and symmetry using information theory.

fr_Ar_OH.label: Number of Aromatic Hydroxyl Groups
fr_Ar_OH.description: The number of aromatic hydroxyl (phenol) functional groups present in the molecule.

fr_ether.label: Number of Ether Groups
fr_ether.description: The number of ether functional groups (R–O–R') present in the molecule.

NHOHCount.label: Number of NH and OH Groups
NHOHCount.description: The number of nitrogen-hydrogen and oxygen-hydrogen groups in the molecule.

NOCount.label: Number of Nitrogen and Oxygen Atoms
NOCount.description: The total number of nitrogen and oxygen atoms in the molecule.

NumAliphaticCarbocycles.label: Number of Aliphatic Carbocycles
NumAliphaticCarbocycles.description: The number of non-aromatic carbon-only rings.

NumAromaticCarbocycles.label: Number of Aromatic Carbocycles
NumAromaticCarbocycles.description: The number of aromatic carbon-only rings.

NumAtomStereoCenters.label: Number of Atom Stereocenters
NumAtomStereoCenters.description: The total number of stereogenic atoms.

NumBridgeheadAtoms.label: Number of Bridgehead Atoms
NumBridgeheadAtoms.description: The number of bridgehead atoms shared between fused ring systems.

NumSpiroAtoms.label: Number of Spiro Atoms
NumSpiroAtoms.description: The number of atoms shared by two rings through a single atom.

NumUnspecifiedAtomStereoCenters.label: Number of Unspecified Atom Stereocenters
NumUnspecifiedAtomStereoCenters.description: The number of stereogenic atoms whose stereochemistry is unspecified.

RingCount.label: Ring Count
RingCount.description: The total number of rings in the molecular graph.

Phi.label: Kier Molecular Flexibility Index
Phi.description: A molecular flexibility descriptor proposed by Kier that combines molecular size, branching, and cyclicity to estimate overall structural flexibility.

MaxPartialCharge.label: Maximum partial atomic charge
MaxPartialCharge.description: Largest computed Gasteiger partial atomic charge.

MinPartialCharge.label: Minimum partial atomic charge
MinPartialCharge.description: Smallest computed Gasteiger partial atomic charge.

MaxAbsPartialCharge.label: Maximum absolute partial charge
MaxAbsPartialCharge.description: Largest absolute value of atomic partial charge.

MinAbsPartialCharge.label: Minimum absolute partial charge
MinAbsPartialCharge.description: Smallest absolute value of atomic partial charge.

NumHeterocycles.label: Number of Heterocyclic Rings
NumHeterocycles.description: Total count of ring systems containing at least one non-carbon atom (e.g. nitrogen, oxygen, sulfur). Includes both aromatic and non-aromatic heterocycles.

NumSaturatedCarbocycles.label: Number of Saturated Carbocycles
NumSaturatedCarbocycles.description: The number of fully saturated rings composed exclusively of carbon atoms. These rings contain no double bonds, no aromatic character, and no heteroatoms, such as cyclohexane-type rings.

Chi0.label: Zeroth-Order Molecular Connectivity Index
Chi0.description: Sum of the reciprocal square roots of the atomic valence degrees for all heavy atoms. Encodes molecular size and atomic connectivity without considering bond topology.

Chi1.label: First-Order Molecular Connectivity Index
Chi1.description: Sum of the reciprocal square roots of the products of atomic degree pairs across all bonds. Encodes information about directly bonded atom pairs and molecular branching.

Chi2v.label: Second-Order Valence-Corrected Molecular Connectivity Index
Chi2v.description: Second-order molecular connectivity index calculated using valence-corrected atomic degrees over all paths of length two. Incorporates heteroatom valence information into two-bond environment encoding.

Chi3v.label: Third-Order Valence-Corrected Molecular Connectivity Index
Chi3v.description: Third-order molecular connectivity index calculated using valence-corrected atomic degrees over all paths of length three. Sensitive to branching patterns and heteroatom placement within three-bond fragments.

Chi4v.label: Fourth-Order Valence-Corrected Molecular Connectivity Index
Chi4v.description: Fourth-order molecular connectivity index calculated using valence-corrected atomic degrees over all paths of length four. Reflects longer-range structural and electronic environment diversity.

fr_Al_COO.label: Aliphatic carboxylic acid
fr_Al_COO.description: Number of aliphatic carboxylic acid groups.

fr_Al_OH_noTert.label: Non-tertiary aliphatic hydroxyl
fr_Al_OH_noTert.description: Number of primary and secondary alcohol groups.

fr_Ar_COO.label: Aromatic carboxylic acid
fr_Ar_COO.description: Number of aromatic carboxylic acid groups.

fr_COO.label: Carboxylic acid
fr_COO.description: Total number of carboxylic acid groups.

fr_COO2.label: Carboxylate
fr_COO2.description: Total number of carboxylate/carboxylic acid groups.

fr_Ar_N.label: Aromatic nitrogen atom
fr_Ar_N.description: Number of aromatic nitrogen atoms in heterocycles.

fr_Ar_NH.label: Aromatic NH
fr_Ar_NH.description: Number of aromatic nitrogen atoms bearing hydrogen.

fr_NH0.label: Tertiary amine
fr_NH0.description: Number of tertiary amine nitrogen atoms.

fr_NH1.label: Secondary amine
fr_NH1.description: Number of secondary amine nitrogen atoms.

fr_NH2.label: Primary amine
fr_NH2.description: Number of primary amine nitrogen atoms.

fr_C_O_noCOO.label: Non-carboxyl carbonyl
fr_C_O_noCOO.description: Carbonyl groups excluding carboxylic acids and esters.

fr_ketone_Topliss.label: Topliss ketone
fr_ketone_Topliss.description: Ketone pattern used in Topliss medicinal chemistry rules.

fr_aryl_methyl.label: Aryl methyl
fr_aryl_methyl.description: Number of methyl groups attached to aromatic rings.

fr_nitro_arom_nonortho.label: Aromatic nitro (non-ortho)
fr_nitro_arom_nonortho.description: Aromatic nitro groups not adjacent to another substituent.

fr_unbrch_alkane.label: Unbranched alkane
fr_unbrch_alkane.description: Presence of an unbranched alkane chain.

fr_para_hydroxylation.label: Para hydroxylation site
fr_para_hydroxylation.description: Potential para-hydroxylation site used in medicinal chemistry.

fr_phenol_noOrthoHbond.label: Phenol without ortho hydrogen bond
fr_phenol_noOrthoHbond.description: Phenol groups lacking intramolecular ortho hydrogen bonding.

fr_phos_acid.label: Phosphonic/phosphoric acid
fr_phos_acid.description: Number of phosphonic or phosphoric acid groups.

fr_allylic_oxid.label: Number of Allylic Oxidation Sites
fr_allylic_oxid.description: Count of allylic carbon positions (sp³ carbons adjacent to a carbon-carbon double bond) that are susceptible to oxidative metabolism. These sites are recognized as potential metabolic soft spots in drug molecules, where cytochrome P450 enzymes can introduce hydroxyl groups.

fr_Imine.label: Number of Imine Groups
fr_Imine.description: Count of imine functional groups (C=N) where the nitrogen bears no hydrogen and is double-bonded to a carbon. Distinct from oximes and hydrazones which carry additional heteroatom substituents.

fr_aniline.label: Number of Aniline Groups
fr_aniline.description: Count of primary aromatic amine groups where an NH2 is directly attached to an aromatic ring. A subclass of aromatic amines relevant to dye chemistry, reactivity, and toxicity screening.

carboxylate.label: Carboxylate Anion
carboxylate.description: Deprotonated carboxylic acid group carrying a formal negative charge on oxygen. Common in physiological conditions and metal coordination.

carboxylate_or_acid.label: Carboxylate or Carboxylic Acid
carboxylate_or_acid.description: SMARTS pattern matching either the protonated carboxylic acid or its deprotonated carboxylate form. Useful for pH-independent detection.

anhydride.label: Acid Anhydride
anhydride.description: Two acyl groups linked through an oxygen atom. Highly reactive electrophile used in acylation reactions and polymer chemistry.

acid_chloride.label: Acid Chloride (Acyl Chloride)
acid_chloride.description: Acyl group bearing a chlorine leaving group. Extremely reactive acylating agent used in synthesis.

quinone.label: Quinone
quinone.description: Cyclic diketone with a conjugated system, typically derived from aromatic rings. Important in redox biology, electron transfer, and natural product chemistry.

hydroxamate.label: Hydroxamate
hydroxamate.description: Functional group containing N–OH adjacent to a carbonyl (RC(=O)NHOH). Strong metal chelator widely used in HDAC inhibitors and siderophores.

thiolate.label: Thiolate Anion
thiolate.description: Deprotonated thiol group carrying a formal negative charge on sulfur. Excellent soft metal ligand and nucleophile in biological systems.

disulfide.label: Disulfide Bond
disulfide.description: Covalent S–S bond between two sulfur atoms. Critical in protein folding, redox biochemistry, and prodrug design.

sulfoxide.label: Sulfoxide
sulfoxide.description: Sulfur atom bearing one oxo group and two carbon substituents (R–S(=O)–R'). Chiral center in many drugs; intermediate oxidation state between sulfide and sulfone.

sulfonic_acid.label: Sulfonic Acid
sulfonic_acid.description: Strongly acidic group (–SO₃H) with sulfur bearing two oxo groups and a hydroxyl. Used in ion exchange resins, dyes, and as bioisostere for carboxylic acids.

sulfonate.label: Sulfonate Anion
sulfonate.description: Deprotonated sulfonic acid (–SO₃⁻). Highly water-soluble anionic group used in dyes, surfactants, and drug formulation.

thiocarbonyl.label: Thiocarbonyl
thiocarbonyl.description: Carbon-sulfur double bond (C=S). Found in thioketones, thioamides, and thioureas; more polarisable than carbonyl analogues.

thioamide.label: Thioamide
thioamide.description: Amide group where the carbonyl oxygen is replaced by sulfur (RC(=S)NR'). Found in antibiotics and used as a carbonyl bioisostere.

thiourea.label: Thiourea
thiourea.description: Urea analogue with a thiocarbonyl group (NH₂–C(=S)–NH₂ scaffold). Used in metal chelation, anion binding, and as a pharmacophore.

phosphonic_acid.label: Phosphonic Acid
phosphonic_acid.description: Organophosphorus group bearing two hydroxyl groups on phosphorus (RP(=O)(OH)₂). Bioisostere for carboxylic and phosphoric acids.

phosphonate.label: Phosphonate
phosphonate.description: Mono- or dianionic form of phosphonic acid. Strong metal binder and substrate mimic in enzyme inhibition.

phosphate.label: Phosphate
phosphate.description: Fully hydroxylated phosphorus oxoacid P(=O)(OH)₃ or its esters/salts. Central to energy metabolism, nucleic acids, and signalling.

phosphate_ester.label: Phosphate Ester
phosphate_ester.description: Phosphate group where one or more hydroxyl is esterified with an alkyl or aryl group. Found in nucleotides, lipids, and prodrugs.

phosphine.label: Phosphine
phosphine.description: Trialkyl or triaryl phosphorus(III) compound (PR₃). Important ligand in transition metal catalysis and organophosphorus chemistry.

phosphine_oxide.label: Phosphine Oxide
phosphine_oxide.description: Phosphorus(V) compound with a P=O bond and three carbon substituents (R₃P=O). Used as a ligand, extractant, and synthetic intermediate.

boronic_acid.label: Boronic Acid
boronic_acid.description: Organoboron compound bearing two hydroxyl groups (RB(OH)₂). Key building block in Suzuki coupling, saccharide sensing, and protease inhibition.

boronate_ester.label: Boronate Ester
boronate_ester.description: Boronic acid where both hydroxyls are esterified with an alcohol or diol (RB(OR')₂). Used as a protecting group and in responsive materials.

silane.label: Silane (Si–H)
silane.description: Silicon atom bearing at least one hydrogen. Used as a reducing agent, coupling agent precursor, and in surface functionalisation.

siloxane.label: Siloxane
siloxane.description: Si–O–Si linkage forming the backbone of polysiloxanes (silicones). Important in materials science, coatings, and biomedical devices.

fluoro.label: Fluorine Substituent
fluoro.description: Any fluorine atom present in the molecule. Strongly influences metabolic stability, lipophilicity, and binding affinity in drug design.

chloro.label: Chlorine Substituent
chloro.description: Any chlorine atom present in the molecule. Common in pharmaceuticals and agrochemicals; affects lipophilicity and electrophilicity.

bromo.label: Bromine Substituent
bromo.description: Any bromine atom present in the molecule. Used in cross-coupling chemistry and as a metabolic stability probe.

iodo.label: Iodine Substituent
iodo.description: Any iodine atom present in the molecule. Used in radiopharmaceuticals, cross-coupling, and as a steric/electronic probe.

alkene.label: Alkene (C=C)
alkene.description: Carbon-carbon double bond between two sp² carbons. Site of electrophilic addition, metathesis, and oxidative metabolism.

alkyne.label: Alkyne (C≡C)
alkyne.description: Carbon-carbon triple bond between two sp carbons. Used in click chemistry, as a metabolic probe, and in materials science.

biphenyl.label: Biphenyl
biphenyl.description: Two directly connected benzene rings. Common scaffold in pharmaceuticals and liquid crystals; can adopt non-planar conformations.

terphenyl.label: Terphenyl
terphenyl.description: Three linearly connected benzene rings. Used in organic electronics, liquid crystals, and as a rigid scaffold.

pyrimidine.label: Pyrimidine Ring
pyrimidine.description: Six-membered aromatic ring with nitrogen atoms at positions 1 and 3. Found in nucleobases (cytosine, thymine, uracil) and many kinase inhibitors.

pyrazine.label: Pyrazine Ring
pyrazine.description: Six-membered aromatic ring with nitrogen atoms at positions 1 and 4. Found in vitamins and used as a ligand in coordination chemistry.

pyridazine.label: Pyridazine Ring
pyridazine.description: Six-membered aromatic ring with adjacent nitrogen atoms at positions 1 and 2. Found in pharmaceuticals and agrochemicals.

triazine.label: Triazine Ring
triazine.description: Six-membered aromatic ring containing three nitrogen atoms. Used in herbicides, dyes, and as a multidentate ligand scaffold.

imidazolate.label: Imidazolate Anion
imidazolate.description: Deprotonated imidazole ring bearing a formal negative charge on nitrogen. Key bridging ligand in zeolitic imidazolate frameworks (ZIFs) and metalloenzyme active sites.

benzimidazole.label: Benzimidazole
benzimidazole.description: Fused bicyclic system of benzene and imidazole rings. Privileged scaffold in pharmaceuticals including antiparasitic and antifungal agents.

pyrazole.label: Pyrazole Ring
pyrazole.description: Five-membered aromatic ring with two adjacent nitrogen atoms, one bearing a hydrogen. Versatile pharmacophore and metal coordination motif.

pyrazolate.label: Pyrazolate Anion
pyrazolate.description: Deprotonated pyrazole ring. Common bridging ligand in metal-organic frameworks and coordination polymers.

triazole.label: Triazole Ring
triazole.description: Five-membered aromatic ring containing three nitrogen atoms. Central to click chemistry (1,2,3-triazole) and antifungal drugs (1,2,4-triazole).

triazolate.label: Triazolate Anion
triazolate.description: Deprotonated triazole ring. Used as a bridging ligand in metal-organic frameworks with high nitrogen content.

tetrazolate.label: Tetrazolate Anion
tetrazolate.description: Deprotonated tetrazole ring. Bioisostere for carboxylate in medicinal chemistry and a high-nitrogen ligand in coordination chemistry.

isoxazole.label: Isoxazole Ring
isoxazole.description: Five-membered aromatic ring with oxygen at position 1 and nitrogen at position 2. Found in antibiotics (cycloserine) and anti-inflammatory drugs.

isothiazole.label: Isothiazole Ring
isothiazole.description: Five-membered aromatic ring with sulfur at position 1 and nitrogen at position 2. Found in biocides and pharmaceuticals.

quinoline.label: Quinoline Ring
quinoline.description: Fused benzene-pyridine bicyclic system with nitrogen at position 1. Found in antimalarials, antibiotics, and metal chelators.

isoquinoline.label: Isoquinoline Ring
isoquinoline.description: Fused benzene-pyridine bicyclic system with nitrogen at position 2. Found in alkaloids and used as a bidentate ligand.

phenanthroline.label: Phenanthroline
phenanthroline.description: Tricyclic aromatic system with two nitrogen atoms. Classical bidentate or tridentate ligand for transition metals; used in luminescent probes.

bipyridine.label: Bipyridine
bipyridine.description: Two pyridine rings connected by a single bond. The most widely used bidentate nitrogen ligand in coordination chemistry and photocatalysis.

terpyridine.label: Terpyridine
terpyridine.description: Three connected pyridine rings forming a tridentate N,N,N-chelating ligand. Widely used in supramolecular chemistry and metallopolymers.

benzofuran.label: Benzofuran
benzofuran.description: Fused benzene-furan bicyclic aromatic system. Found in natural products and used as a scaffold in medicinal chemistry.

benzothiophene.label: Benzothiophene
benzothiophene.description: Fused benzene-thiophene bicyclic aromatic system. Found in pharmaceuticals including raloxifene and used in organic electronics.

pyridinium.label: Pyridinium Cation
pyridinium.description: Positively charged protonated or N-alkylated pyridine ring. Found in ionic liquids, cofactors (NAD⁺), and antimicrobial agents.

imidazolium.label: Imidazolium Cation
imidazolium.description: Positively charged N-substituted imidazole ring. The defining cation of imidazolium ionic liquids and N-heterocyclic carbene precursors.

sulfonium.label: Sulfonium Cation
sulfonium.description: Positively charged trivalent sulfur species (R₃S⁺). Found in biological methyl donors (S-adenosylmethionine) and as reactive intermediates.

phosphonium.label: Phosphonium Cation
phosphonium.description: Positively charged tetravalent phosphorus species (R₄P⁺). Used in Wittig reagents, mitochondria-targeting drug delivery, and ionic liquids.

peroxide.label: Peroxide
peroxide.description: O–O single bond between two oxygen atoms. Highly reactive oxidant; found in antimalarials (artemisinin) and used as a radical initiator.

beta_diketone.label: Beta-Diketone
beta_diketone.description: Two ketone groups separated by a single methylene or methine. Forms stable six-membered chelate rings with metal ions; found in acetylacetone and curcumin.

acetylacetonate_like.label: Acetylacetonate-like (Enolate Form)
acetylacetonate_like.description: Deprotonated beta-diketone in its enolate form. Classical monoanionic O,O-bidentate chelating ligand for a wide range of metal ions.

salicylaldehyde.label: Salicylaldehyde
salicylaldehyde.description: 2-Hydroxybenzaldehyde motif combining a phenol and an aldehyde in ortho positions. Key precursor to Schiff base ligands and salen complexes.

salen_like.label: Salen-like Schiff Base
salen_like.description: Imine nitrogen adjacent to a phenol hydroxyl forming an N,O-chelating unit. Core motif of salen ligands used in asymmetric catalysis.

diimine.label: Alpha-Diimine
diimine.description: Two imine groups (C=N) on adjacent or nearby carbons forming a bidentate N,N-chelating unit. Common in olefin polymerisation catalysts.

dicarboxylate.label: Dicarboxylate
dicarboxylate.description: Two carboxylate or carboxylic acid groups within the same molecule. Key linker in metal-organic frameworks and coordination polymers.

hydroxy_carboxylate.label: Hydroxy Carboxylate
hydroxy_carboxylate.description: Molecule containing both a hydroxyl and a carboxylate group enabling O,O-bidentate chelation. Found in citrate, malate, and alpha-hydroxy acids.

amino_carboxylate.label: Amino Carboxylate
amino_carboxylate.description: Molecule containing both an amine and a carboxylate group enabling N,O-chelation. Central motif in amino acids, EDTA, and NTA.

amidoxime.label: Amidoxime
amidoxime.description: Functional group combining an amide nitrogen and an oxime (C(=NOH)NH₂). Strong chelator for uranyl and other actinide ions; used in seawater uranium extraction.

catechol.label: Catechol (1,2-Dihydroxybenzene)
catechol.description: Benzene ring bearing two adjacent hydroxyl groups. Potent O,O-chelating unit for hard metal ions; found in siderophores and DOPA-based adhesives.

resorcinol.label: Resorcinol (1,3-Dihydroxybenzene)
resorcinol.description: Benzene ring bearing two hydroxyl groups in meta positions. Used in resins, pharmaceuticals, and as a chelating scaffold.

hydroquinone.label: Hydroquinone (1,4-Dihydroxybenzene)
hydroquinone.description: Benzene ring bearing two hydroxyl groups in para positions. Reversible redox couple with quinone; used in photography, antioxidants, and electrochemistry.

oxamate_like.label: Oxamate-like Group
oxamate_like.description: Amide of oxalic acid bearing a carboxylate or carboxylic acid (NH₂–C(=O)–COO⁻). Bidentate N,O-chelating group used in coordination chemistry.

hydrazide.label: Hydrazide
hydrazide.description: Acyl hydrazine group (RC(=O)–NH–NH₂). Forms hydrazone Schiff bases with aldehydes and ketones; used as a metal chelating pharmacophore.

zwitterion.label: Zwitterion
zwitterion.description: Molecule bearing both a formal positive and a formal negative charge. Includes amino acids, betaines, and many drug molecules at physiological pH.

carbonate_ester.label: Carbonate Ester
carbonate_ester.description: Ester of carbonic acid (O=C(OR)(OR')). Used as a protecting group, in polycarbonate polymers, and as a prodrug linker.

carbamate.label: Carbamate (Urethane)
carbamate.description: Ester of carbamic acid (NR–C(=O)–OR'). Widely used in prodrug design, protecting group chemistry (Boc, Cbz), and polyurethane polymers.

carbamic_acid.label: Carbamic Acid
carbamic_acid.description: Free acid form of carbamate (NR–C(=O)–OH). Thermally labile; important intermediate in CO₂ fixation and amine protection.

hemiaminal.label: Hemiaminal
hemiaminal.description: Carbon bearing both an OH and an NR₂ group. Unstable intermediate in imine formation and in the mechanism of many enzymatic reactions.

hemiketal.label: Hemiketal
hemiketal.description: Carbon bearing both an OH and an OR group adjacent to another carbon. Intermediate in ketal formation; found in cyclic sugar forms.

acetal.label: Acetal
acetal.description: Carbon bearing two OR groups derived from an aldehyde (CH(OR)₂). Stable protecting group for aldehydes under basic conditions.

orthoester.label: Orthoester
orthoester.description: Carbon bearing three OR groups (C(OR)₃). Used as a protecting group and as a slow-release linker in prodrug and polymer chemistry.

enol.label: Enol
enol.description: Hydroxyl group directly attached to a C=C double bond (C=C–OH). Tautomeric form of carbonyl compounds; important in enzyme mechanisms.

enol_ether.label: Enol Ether
enol_ether.description: Oxygen atom bridging a vinyl carbon and an alkyl group (C=C–OR). Used as a protecting group and as a nucleophilic alkene in synthesis.

vinyl_halide.label: Vinyl Halide
vinyl_halide.description: Halogen substituent directly attached to an sp² carbon of a C=C bond. Substrate in cross-coupling reactions (Heck, Suzuki, Negishi).

allyl_halide.label: Allyl Halide
allyl_halide.description: Halogen on a carbon adjacent to a C=C bond. Reactive electrophile in allylic substitution and palladium-catalysed coupling.

alpha_halo_carbonyl.label: Alpha-Halo Carbonyl
alpha_halo_carbonyl.description: Halogen atom on the carbon alpha to a carbonyl group. Potent electrophile and alkylating agent; used in synthesis and as a warhead in covalent drugs.

michael_acceptor.label: Michael Acceptor
michael_acceptor.description: Electron-deficient alkene conjugated with a carbonyl group (enone). Reacts with nucleophiles via 1,4-addition; used as a covalent warhead in drug design.

maleimide.label: Maleimide
maleimide.description: Cyclic imide with a C=C double bond in the ring. Highly reactive Michael acceptor used in bioconjugation and polymer chemistry.

succinimide.label: Succinimide
succinimide.description: Five-membered cyclic imide derived from succinic acid. Found in drugs and as a byproduct of maleimide–thiol conjugation.

phthalimide.label: Phthalimide
phthalimide.description: Cyclic imide fused to a benzene ring. Used as a nitrogen protecting group (Gabriel synthesis) and in thalidomide-related pharmacology.

beta_lactam.label: Beta-Lactam
beta_lactam.description: Four-membered cyclic amide. Core pharmacophore of penicillin and cephalosporin antibiotics; reacts covalently with bacterial transpeptidases.

sultam.label: Sultam
sultam.description: Cyclic sulfonamide where nitrogen and sulfur are part of the same ring. Found in pharmaceutical agents and used as a chiral auxiliary.

sultone.label: Sultone
sultone.description: Cyclic sulfonate ester. Reactive electrophile used as an alkylating agent and in battery electrolyte additives.

dioxolane.label: Dioxolane
dioxolane.description: Five-membered ring containing two oxygen atoms and three carbons. Used as a cyclic acetal protecting group for aldehydes and ketones.

crown_ether_fragment.label: Crown Ether Fragment
crown_ether_fragment.description: Repeating ethyleneoxy unit (–CH₂CH₂O–) characteristic of crown ethers. Selectively binds alkali and alkaline earth metal cations based on cavity size.

cryptand_fragment.label: Cryptand Fragment
cryptand_fragment.description: Branched polyether-polyamine unit forming three-dimensional cavities for metal ion encapsulation. Provides stronger binding than crown ethers.

amino_acid_backbone.label: Amino Acid Backbone
amino_acid_backbone.description: The canonical N–Cα–C(=O)–O motif of an alpha-amino acid. Detected regardless of side chain identity; used to identify amino acid residues.

peptide_bond.label: Peptide Bond
peptide_bond.description: Amide bond connecting adjacent amino acid residues (–NH–C(=O)–CH–NH–). Fundamental linkage in proteins and peptide-based drugs.

nucleobase_purine.label: Purine Nucleobase
nucleobase_purine.description: Fused imidazole-pyrimidine bicyclic system. Core scaffold of adenine and guanine; found in nucleic acids and purinergic signalling molecules.

nucleobase_pyrimidine_like.label: Pyrimidine Nucleobase-like
nucleobase_pyrimidine_like.description: Pyrimidine ring bearing a carbonyl group mimicking cytosine, thymine, or uracil. Detected as a nucleobase-related scaffold.

catecholate.label: Catecholate Dianion
catecholate.description: Fully deprotonated catechol bearing two negative charges on adjacent phenolic oxygens. Extremely strong chelating ligand for hard metal ions.

semiquinone.label: Semiquinone Radical
semiquinone.description: One-electron reduction product of a quinone bearing a radical and a hydroxyl group. Important intermediate in biological electron transfer chains.

phenolate.label: Phenolate Anion
phenolate.description: Deprotonated phenol bearing a formal negative charge on oxygen (ArO⁻). Metal binding group and reactive nucleophile in enzymatic reactions.

alkoxide.label: Alkoxide Anion
alkoxide.description: Deprotonated aliphatic alcohol (RO⁻). Strong base and nucleophile; common metal-binding group in alkoxide metal complexes.

enolate.label: Enolate Anion
enolate.description: Deprotonated enol carrying a negative charge delocalised over C–C–O. Key reactive intermediate in aldol reactions and enzymatic carbon–carbon bond formation.

thiocarbamate.label: Thiocarbamate
thiocarbamate.description: Carbamate where the carbonyl oxygen is replaced by sulfur (NR–C(=S)–OR'). Used in pesticides, vulcanisation accelerators, and as a metal chelator.

dithiocarbamate.label: Dithiocarbamate
dithiocarbamate.description: Both oxygens of carbamate replaced by sulfur (NR–C(=S)–S⁻). Potent soft metal chelator used in fungicides, rubber vulcanisation, and heavy metal remediation.

xanthate.label: Xanthate
xanthate.description: Ester of xanthic acid (RO–C(=S)–S⁻). Used in froth flotation for metal ore processing and as a precursor to dithioesters in RAFT polymerisation.

trithiocarbonate.label: Trithiocarbonate
trithiocarbonate.description: All-sulfur analogue of carbonate (S–C(=S)–S). Used as a RAFT agent in controlled radical polymerisation.

phosphoramide.label: Phosphoramide
phosphoramide.description: Phosphorus bearing two amino groups and one oxo group (P(=O)(NR₂)₂). Found in chemotherapy agents (cyclophosphamide-like) and as a Lewis base ligand.

phosphoramidate.label: Phosphoramidate
phosphoramidate.description: Phosphate ester where one oxygen is replaced by nitrogen (P(=O)(NR)(OR)(OR)). Used in nucleotide prodrugs (ProTide technology).

thiophosphate.label: Thiophosphate
thiophosphate.description: Phosphate group where one oxo oxygen is replaced by sulfur (P(=S)(OH)₃ or ester). Found in insecticides and in phosphorothioate antisense oligonucleotides.

arsenic_acid.label: Arsenic Acid / Arsenate
arsenic_acid.description: Organoarsenic group bearing hydroxyl groups on arsenic (As(=O)(OH)₂ or similar). Found in arsenical drugs and as a phosphate mimic in toxicology.

selenol.label: Selenol
selenol.description: Se–H group analogous to a thiol. Found in selenocysteine, the 21st amino acid; strongly nucleophilic and redox-active.

selenide.label: Selenide
selenide.description: Divalent selenium bonded to two carbon groups (R–Se–R'). Organoselenium compound with antioxidant and catalytic properties.

diselenide.label: Diselenide
diselenide.description: Se–Se bond analogous to disulfide. Found in selenoprotein mimetics and used in redox-responsive drug delivery systems.

selenoxide.label: Selenoxide
selenoxide.description: Selenium bearing one oxo group (R–Se(=O)–R'). Undergoes facile elimination to give alkenes; intermediate in biological selenium oxidation.

telluride.label: Telluride
telluride.description: Divalent tellurium bonded to two carbon groups (R–Te–R'). Used in organotellurium chemistry and as a precursor in semiconductor synthesis.

metal_binding_NNO.label: Tridentate N,N,O Metal-Binding Motif
metal_binding_NNO.description: Fragment containing two nitrogen donors and one oxygen donor in proximity, capable of forming a facial or meridional tridentate chelate with metal ions.

metal_binding_ONO.label: Tridentate O,N,O Metal-Binding Motif
metal_binding_ONO.description: Fragment containing two oxygen donors flanking a nitrogen donor, forming a pincer-type tridentate chelate. Found in aminocarboxylate and hydroxamate-based ligands.

metal_binding_NNN.label: Tridentate N,N,N Metal-Binding Motif
metal_binding_NNN.description: Fragment containing three nitrogen donors in proximity capable of meridional or facial tridentate chelation. Found in terpyridine, tren, and triazacyclononane scaffolds.

porphyrin_fragment.label: Porphyrin Fragment
porphyrin_fragment.description: Aromatic macrocyclic core with alternating pyrrole and methine units. Tetradentate N₄ ligand that binds iron in haem, magnesium in chlorophyll, and cobalt in vitamin B₁₂.

macrocycle_fragment.label: Macrocyclic Ring (≥14-membered)
macrocycle_fragment.description: Ring system with 14 or more atoms. Macrocycles exhibit unique conformational properties and are increasingly important in drug discovery for targeting protein-protein interactions.

rotaxane_stopper.label: Rotaxane Stopper Group
rotaxane_stopper.description: Bulky trityl-like group used to cap the axle of a rotaxane and prevent dethreading of the macrocyclic wheel. Key component in mechanically interlocked molecules.

catenane_fragment.label: Catenane Ring Fragment
catenane_fragment.description: Large polyether ring characteristic of catenane components. Mechanically interlocked with another ring without covalent bonding.

fluorophore_coumarin.label: Coumarin Fluorophore
fluorophore_coumarin.description: Benzopyranone aromatic system that is intrinsically fluorescent. Widely used as a fluorescent probe, laser dye, and in FRET-based biosensors.

fluorophore_naphthalimide.label: Naphthalimide Fluorophore
fluorophore_naphthalimide.description: Naphthalene bisimide-derived fluorophore with strong absorption and emission. Used in fluorescent probes for ions, pH sensors, and organic semiconductors.

spin_label_nitroxide.label: Nitroxide Spin Label
spin_label_nitroxide.description: Stable N–O radical used as a spin label in EPR spectroscopy. Attached to biomolecules to probe structure and dynamics.

radical_trap_tempo.label: TEMPO Radical Trap
radical_trap_tempo.description: 2,2,6,6-Tetramethylpiperidine-1-oxyl (TEMPO) motif. Persistent nitroxide radical used as an oxidant catalyst, radical trap, and spin label.

photoswitch_azobenzene.label: Azobenzene Photoswitch
photoswitch_azobenzene.description: Trans/cis-isomerisable azo group flanked by phenyl rings. The most widely used molecular photoswitch for light-controlled drug delivery and materials.

photoswitch_diarylethene.label: Diarylethene Photoswitch
photoswitch_diarylethene.description: Photochromic unit based on two aryl groups (typically thiophene) connected by an ethene bridge undergoing reversible electrocyclic ring closure.

redox_ferrocene_like.label: Ferrocene-like Redox Centre
redox_ferrocene_like.description: Iron atom present in an organometallic context. Detected as a proxy for ferrocene-type sandwich complexes with reversible Fe²⁺/Fe³⁺ redox activity.

cage_compound.label: Cage Compound Fragment
cage_compound.description: Presence of strained small rings (3- or 4-membered) in proximity, indicative of cage-like polycyclic frameworks such as cubane, adamantane, or prismane.

cucurbituril_fragment.label: Cucurbituril Fragment
cucurbituril_fragment.description: Glycoluril-derived unit with flanking methylene bridges characteristic of cucurbit[n]uril macrocycles. Host molecules for hydrophobic guest binding in water.

cyclodextrin_fragment.label: Cyclodextrin Fragment
cyclodextrin_fragment.description: Glucopyranose unit with hydroxyl groups characteristic of cyclodextrin macrocycles. Cyclic oligosaccharides used for drug encapsulation and chiral separation.

carboxylic_acid.label: Carboxylic Acid
carboxylic_acid.description: Organic acid group containing a carbonyl and hydroxyl on the same carbon (–COOH). Fundamental functional group in biochemistry, pharmaceuticals, and materials chemistry.

ester.label: Ester
ester.description: Carbonyl group flanked by an oxygen connected to a carbon (RC(=O)OR'). Widely found in fats, fragrances, and polymers; commonly used as a prodrug linker.

amide.label: Amide
amide.description: Carbonyl group directly bonded to a nitrogen atom (RC(=O)NR'R''). The fundamental linkage in proteins and a key pharmacophore in drug design.

imide.label: Imide
imide.description: Two carbonyl groups flanking a single nitrogen atom (RC(=O)NR'C(=O)R''). Found in phthalimide, succinimide, and maleimide scaffolds.

aldehyde.label: Aldehyde
aldehyde.description: Terminal carbonyl group bearing one hydrogen (RCHO). Reactive electrophile that forms Schiff bases with amines; important in metabolic reactions and covalent drug design.

ketone.label: Ketone
ketone.description: Internal carbonyl group flanked by two carbon substituents (RC(=O)R'). Common pharmacophore and metabolic product of secondary alcohols.

amine_primary.label: Primary Amine
amine_primary.description: Nitrogen bearing two hydrogens and one carbon substituent (RNH₂). Basic and nucleophilic; found in amino acids and many drug molecules.

amine_secondary.label: Secondary Amine
amine_secondary.description: Nitrogen bearing one hydrogen and two carbon substituents (R₂NH). Common in alkaloids, drug scaffolds, and N-dealkylation metabolic pathways.

amine_tertiary.label: Tertiary Amine
amine_tertiary.description: Nitrogen bearing three carbon substituents and no hydrogens (R₃N). Found in many drugs; site of N-oxidation metabolism and quaternisation.

aromatic_amine.label: Aromatic Amine
aromatic_amine.description: Amine nitrogen directly attached to an aromatic ring. Influences basicity, reactivity, and metabolic profile; relevant to toxicity screening.

aniline.label: Aniline
aniline.description: Primary amine directly attached to a benzene or aromatic ring (ArNH₂). Important in dye synthesis and pharmaceuticals; monitored for potential toxic metabolites.

imine.label: Imine (Schiff Base)
imine.description: Carbon-nitrogen double bond (C=N) where nitrogen bears no hydrogen. Forms reversibly from aldehydes or ketones with primary amines; used in metal chelation and covalent drug design.

oxime.label: Oxime
oxime.description: Imine derivative where nitrogen bears a hydroxyl group (C=NOH). Formed from carbonyl compounds and hydroxylamine; used as a protecting group and in reactivation of nerve agent inhibited enzymes.

hydrazone.label: Hydrazone
hydrazone.description: Imine derivative where nitrogen is connected to another nitrogen (C=N–NR₂). Formed from carbonyl compounds and hydrazines; used as a pH-responsive linker in drug delivery.

azide.label: Azide
azide.description: Three consecutive nitrogen atoms (–N₃) in a linear arrangement. Used in click chemistry (Huisgen cycloaddition), bioorthogonal labelling, and as a high-energy functional group.

azo.label: Azo Group
azo.description: Nitrogen-nitrogen double bond flanked by two carbon groups (R–N=N–R'). Found in azo dyes, photoswitches, and prodrug linkers cleaved by azo-reductases.

nitro.label: Nitro Group
nitro.description: Nitrogen bearing two oxygens in a –NO₂ arrangement. Strongly electron-withdrawing; found in antibiotics and explosives and reduced metabolically to amines.

nitrile.label: Nitrile (Cyano Group)
nitrile.description: Carbon-nitrogen triple bond (C≡N). Metabolically stable bioisostere for carboxylic acids and amides; used in kinase inhibitors and as a synthetic intermediate.

isocyanate.label: Isocyanate
isocyanate.description: Highly reactive group containing a cumulated N=C=O system. Reacts rapidly with amines and alcohols; used in polyurethane synthesis and as a covalent warhead.

isothiocyanate.label: Isothiocyanate
isothiocyanate.description: Cumulated N=C=S system analogous to isocyanate. Found in glucosinolate-derived natural products such as sulforaphane; reacts with amines and thiols.

urea.label: Urea
urea.description: Two amine groups flanking a carbonyl (H₂N–C(=O)–NH₂ scaffold). Versatile hydrogen-bond donor and acceptor; found in many kinase inhibitors and used as a solubilising group.

guanidine.label: Guanidine
guanidine.description: Strongly basic group with three nitrogens on a central carbon (HN=C(NH₂)₂). Found in arginine, metformin, and many natural products; forms strong hydrogen bonds.

alcohol.label: Alcohol
alcohol.description: Hydroxyl group attached to an sp³ carbon (ROH). Ubiquitous in natural products and drugs; site of glucuronidation and oxidative metabolism.

phenol.label: Phenol
phenol.description: Hydroxyl group directly attached to an aromatic ring (ArOH). More acidic than aliphatic alcohols; site of glucuronidation and sulfation metabolism.

ether.label: Ether
ether.description: Oxygen atom bridging two carbon groups (R–O–R'). Metabolically stable linker; found in many drug scaffolds and used as a protecting group.

epoxide.label: Epoxide (Oxirane)
epoxide.description: Three-membered ring containing one oxygen and two carbons. Highly reactive electrophile; formed as a metabolic intermediate and used as a synthetic building block.

thiol.label: Thiol
thiol.description: Sulfhydryl group (–SH) attached to a carbon. Strong nucleophile and soft metal ligand; found in cysteine residues and used in bioconjugation chemistry.

thioether.label: Thioether (Sulfide)
thioether.description: Sulfur atom bonded to two carbon groups (R–S–R'). Found in methionine and many drugs; oxidised metabolically to sulfoxides and sulfones.

sulfone.label: Sulfone
sulfone.description: Sulfur bearing two oxo groups and two carbon substituents (R–S(=O)₂–R'). Metabolically stable; used as a bioisostere and to improve aqueous solubility.

sulfonamide.label: Sulfonamide
sulfonamide.description: Sulfonyl group bearing an amine (R–S(=O)₂–NR'R''). Found in antibiotics, diuretics, and antidiabetics; a key bioisostere for carboxylic acids.

alkyl_halide.label: Alkyl Halide
alkyl_halide.description: Halogen substituent on an sp³ carbon. Reactive electrophile used in alkylation reactions; relevant to toxicity and reactivity screening.

aryl_halide.label: Aryl Halide
aryl_halide.description: Halogen substituent directly attached to an aromatic ring. Key substrate in cross-coupling reactions (Suzuki, Heck, Buchwald); influences lipophilicity and metabolic stability.

halogen.label: Halogen Substituent (Any)
halogen.description: Any fluorine, chlorine, bromine, or iodine atom present in the molecule regardless of attachment context. Used as a broad-spectrum halogen count.

benzene.label: Benzene Ring
benzene.description: Six-membered fully aromatic carbocycle (C₆H₆ scaffold). The most common aromatic ring in pharmaceuticals; influences lipophilicity, metabolic hydroxylation, and stacking interactions.

furan.label: Furan Ring
furan.description: Five-membered aromatic ring containing one oxygen atom. Found in natural products and drug scaffolds; can be metabolically activated to reactive epoxide intermediates.

thiophene.label: Thiophene Ring
thiophene.description: Five-membered aromatic ring containing one sulfur atom. Common bioisostere for benzene and furan; found in many drugs including clopidogrel and tiotropium.

thiazole.label: Thiazole Ring
thiazole.description: Five-membered aromatic ring containing one sulfur and one nitrogen atom. Found in vitamin B₁ and many antibiotics; used as a pharmacophore in medicinal chemistry.

imidazole.label: Imidazole Ring
imidazole.description: Five-membered aromatic ring with two nitrogen atoms, one bearing a hydrogen. Found in histidine, histamine, and many drugs; amphoteric and metal-coordinating.

oxazole.label: Oxazole Ring
oxazole.description: Five-membered aromatic ring containing one oxygen and one nitrogen atom. Found in natural products and used as a carboxylic acid bioisostere in medicinal chemistry.

pyridine.label: Pyridine Ring
pyridine.description: Six-membered aromatic ring containing one nitrogen atom. The most prevalent nitrogen heterocycle in approved drugs; influences basicity, solubility, and metabolism.

tetrazole.label: Tetrazole Ring
tetrazole.description: Five-membered aromatic ring containing four nitrogen atoms and one carbon. Classical bioisostere for carboxylic acids; used in angiotensin receptor blockers (e.g. losartan).

lactone.label: Lactone
lactone.description: Cyclic ester formed by intramolecular esterification. Found in many natural products and macrolide antibiotics; can be hydrolysed under physiological conditions.

aromatic_ring.label: Aromatic Ring (Any)
aromatic_ring.description: Any aromatic ring system detected by a generic aromatic SMARTS pattern. Covers all aromatic carbocycles and heterocycles regardless of heteroatom content.

fr_standalone.fr_Al_OH_noTert: Aliphatic hydroxyl groups excluding tertiary alcohols
fr_standalone.fr_phenol_noOrthoHbond: Phenols excluding ortho intramolecular H-bonding cases
